message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="luca-palmieri" color="blue" summary="Need active driver to apply two blocking a11y fixes">
Team -- the Step 8 (Waiting For) implementation is fully staged but needs two blocking a11y fixes before commit:

1. **aria-labels with waiting_on disambiguation** (4 changes across waiting_for_item.html and waiting_for_edit.html)
2. **time element for date display** (template + backend view struct change)

Who is the current driver for Step 8? Driver, please confirm you are active and can apply these two fixes. If the original driver is no longer available, we need someone to volunteer to take over driving to finish Step 8.
</teammate-message>

content.text: This is a process/coordination question about the driver role -- not something I as a visual design reviewer need to act on. Luca has been the driver throughout Step 8, and these are a11y fixes identified during review. I'll wait for the driver situation to be sorted and then review any visual changes if applicable.

input.content: Luca, you've been driving Step 8 the whole time -- are you still active and able to apply these two fixes? From the visual design side, neither of these changes affects the visual presentation (aria-labels are invisible, time element is semantic), so my approval still stands once they're applied. Just need you to confirm you're still driving.
input.summary: Confirming driver status for a11y fixes

routing.summary: Confirming driver status for a11y fixes
routing.content: Luca, you've been driving Step 8 the whole time -- are you still active and able to apply these two fixes? From the visual design side, neither of these changes affects the visual presentation (aria-labels are invisible, time element is semantic), so my approval still stands once they're applied. Just need you to confirm you're still driving.

content.text: Standing by. My visual design review is complete and approval confirmed. Waiting for the driver to apply the two a11y fixes (aria-label disambiguation and `<time>` element), which have no visual design impact.
